concerns.text: T014 implements 'Filter top complexity issues as "Hard" (avoiding tautology of using `initial_coverage`)' which directly contradicts FR-001 and the Spec's definition of 'Hard Instance' as the bottom [deferred] of initial coverage scores. This task silently relaxes the core experimental definition mandated by the spec, violating the constraint that tasks must not weaken FRs.

T014 description states 'Calculate Cyclomatic Complexity... Filter top complexity issues' but FR-001 and FR-010 explicitly require filtering by 'initial coverage scores' and validating that 'low coverage correlates with genuine ambiguity'. The task replaces the required metric (coverage) with an unmandated proxy (complexity) without spec authorization, constituting a semantic weakening of the requirement.

T031 implements 'Survival Analysis (Cox) or modified Wilcoxon' but fails to explicitly include the 'Bonferroni correction' mandated by SC-004 and FR-006 for the family of tests. While the plan mentions Bonferroni, the task description omits this specific constraint, risking a deliverable that satisfies the statistical test but fails the multiplicity correction requirement.

T034 'Generate `paper/` draft' lacks the explicit instruction to frame findings as 'associational differences' as mandated by FR-007. Without this constraint in the task description, the generated text may inadvertently make causal claims, violating the scientific integrity constraint of the spec.

T015 hardcodes 'manual inspection of 10 random hard issues' while FR-010 requires inspecting a '[deferred] random subset'. Hardcoding a specific number (10) in the task when the spec defers the value creates a rigidity that violates the spec's intent to allow the value to be determined later, potentially locking in a sample size that doesn't match the final dataset scale.
